questions.question: You need to notify multiple dashboard components whenever a stock price changes, without the price source knowing which dashboards exist. Which pattern is the best fit?
questions.explanation: Observer creates a one-to-many dependency: the subject emits events and any number of listeners subscribe independently. Mediator centralizes communication between objects, which adds coupling that isn't needed here.

questions.question: A payment processor needs to support credit cards, PayPal, and crypto — and the payment method must be swappable at runtime without changing the processor code. Which pattern applies?
questions.explanation: Strategy encapsulates interchangeable algorithms behind a common interface and lets you swap them at runtime. Factory Method creates objects but doesn't handle the algorithm-swapping concern.

questions.question: You're building a text editor with undo/redo. Each operation must be stored so it can be reversed. Which pattern treats operations as reversible objects?
questions.explanation: Command encapsulates a request as an object with both execute() and undo() methods, enabling undo/redo stacks. Memento saves and restores state snapshots but doesn't represent the operation itself.

questions.question: Which GoF pattern is best replaced by dependency injection in modern code?
questions.explanation: Singleton hides global state, makes testing hard, and breaks in concurrent environments. DI passes the needed instance explicitly, giving you the same 'single instance' guarantee without the hidden coupling.

questions.question: Your app uses a UserRepository interface, but you must integrate a legacy service that returns LegacyUser objects with different field names. Which pattern bridges the gap?
questions.explanation: Adapter converts one interface into another that the client expects, letting incompatible systems cooperate. Decorator adds behavior; Strategy swaps algorithms; Proxy controls access — none of them translate interface shapes.

questions.question: In Python, what language feature makes the Iterator pattern nearly trivial to implement?
questions.explanation: Python's generator functions (yield/yield from) automatically implement the iterator protocol (__iter__/__next__), producing lazy sequences without exposing internal structure. No class hierarchy needed.
